articles.title: EXCLUSIVE: "Replacing Replaced Image!" — NCERT Chief U-Turns On 'Dancing Girl' Textbook Controversy
articles.description: Following an intense, nationwide debate over historical representation and visual censorship in school curricula, the National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT) has officially decided to step back. Speaking exclusively to CNN-News18’s Nabila Jamal, NCERT Director Professor Dinesh Prasad Saklani broken his silence on the massive row surrounding the retouched image of the iconic Indus Valley Civilisation artifact. -newsn18oc_indiaNews18 Mobile App - https://onelink.to/desc-youtube

articles.title: American billionaire Bill Ackman writes long post slamming Bernie Sanders’ attack on Elon Musk becoming world’s first trillionaire: ‘Your socialism would never...’
articles.description: Billionaire investor Bill Ackman lauded Elon Musk's trillionaire status, achieved through SpaceX's IPO, countering Senator Bernie Sanders' criticism. Ackman argued Musk's ventures, like Starlink, bring global benefits such as low-cost internet and educational access, driving economic growth and innovation. He emphasized Musk's wealth is tied to company value, not cash, and that such entrepreneurs deserve appreciation for advancing humanity.

articles.title: Dia Mirza's environmental remark on patriarchy sparks backlash; internet says 'Aadmi hona paap ho gaya hai'. Here's what she said

While critics argue her statement oversimplifies a complex global crisis and unfairly blames men, supporters contend she was referencing historical power structures and initiating a broader discussion on gender and accountability.

articles.title: Bengal Education Scam: Abhishek Banerjee Appears Before ED in Teacher Recruitment Probe
articles.description: A major political development unfolded in Kolkata as Trinamool Congress (TMC) MP Abhishek Banerjee appeared before the Enforcement Directorate (ED) for questioning in connection with the alleged teacher recruitment scam in West Bengal. According to officials, Banerjee reached the ED office shortly before the scheduled questioning session. The central agency had earlier issued a summons seeking his appearance as part of its ongoing money laundering investigation linked to alleged irregularities in recruitment processes within the state's education sector. The investigation stems from allegations that recruitment rules were violated, resulting in deserving candidates being denied jobs while allegedly ineligible candidates secured appointments through illegal means. The ED has been probing the financial trail associated with the alleged scam and claims to have already identified and attached assets believed to be linked to proceeds of crime. The case has remained one of the most high-profile corruption investigations in West Bengal. Previous stages of the probe saw significant recoveries of cash, gold, and documents during raids conducted on individuals linked to the recruitment process. Investigators are examining whether influential political and administrative figures benefited from the alleged irregularities. Apart from the ED inquiry, Banerjee is also facing scrutiny in a separate investigation related to allegations involving forged signatures, which is being examined by state agencies. The teacher recruitment controversy has triggered intense political confrontation between the ruling TMC and opposition parties, with critics demanding accountability and transparency in public sector hiring. Meanwhile, TMC leaders have repeatedly alleged political targeting by central agencies. With fresh questioning underway, political observers are closely watching the developments, as the probe could have significant implications for West Bengal's political landscape ahead of future electoral contests.

articles.title: US-Iran peace deal a 'temporary ceasefire' before polls: Expert
articles.description: JCFA CEO Sagiv Steinberg calls the announced US-Iran peace deal a 'temporary ceasefire' for US elections. He says Washington achieved none of its goals, while Iran got everything it wanted, including money to revive its proxies and no nuclear deal.

articles.title: Punjab Elections Could Be Held In November This Year, Claims Kejriwal Amid Early Poll Buzz
articles.description: AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal has claimed that Punjab Assembly elections may be advanced to November 2026 instead of being held in February 2027 as scheduled. Kejriwal said he had received information suggesting that the poll schedule could be brought forward, sparking fresh political speculation in the state. The claim comes amid reports that major political parties, including AAP, Congress and BJP, have already begun preparations for a possible early election. While no official announcement has been made, the buzz around advancing the Punjab polls has intensified political activity across the state.

articles.title: US Iran war: Israeli military on alert, preparing for 'potential' incoming fire after Beirut strike
articles.description: US-Iran war news: Israel's military is on high alert. They anticipate a retaliatory strike within hours. This comes after an Israeli strike on Beirut's southern suburbs earlier Sunday. The military is preparing for potential fire towards Israeli territory. The exact origin of the expected attack remains unspecified.

articles.title: PM Modi ensuring no supply disruption amid global risks: Sitharaman
articles.description: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman said despite global risks from crude prices and West Asia tensions, the Modi govt has ensured no supply disruptions. She highlighted India's status as the fastest-growing economy and key welfare schemes.

articles.title: Iran Fires Warning Shot At Trump After Beirut Bombing; Issues Big Order To IRGC; 'STOP NETANYAHU...'
articles.description: The delicate, backchannel peace negotiations between Washington and Tehran are hanging by a thread, following an ultimatum issued by the Iranian Parliament’s National Security Commission. Tehran has explicitly conditioned the survival of the Pakistan-mediated diplomatic track on the United States’ ability to restrain its regional partner, Israel. Following an unprovoked Israel Defense Forces airstrike on a residential neighborhood in southern Beirut, Iranian official Ebrahim Rezaei warned that any broader regional security understanding is "mathematically impossible" while Tel Aviv violates the active, US-managed ceasefire framework. Shifting the strategic burden entirely onto Washington, Tehran declared that if President Trump expects an implementable accord, he must discipline his ally—warning that Iran's missile divisions are fully mobilized and postured to deliver a painful defensive response should these unilateral incursions continue.

articles.title: INS Sharda strengthens India-Sri Lanka maritime ties with port call
articles.description: INS Sharda departed Colombo after a port call that strengthened maritime ties between India and Sri Lanka through training and exchanges. The ship also deployed to the Maldives for a Humanitarian Assistance and Disaster Relief (HADR) exercise.
